questions.question: In ancient Babylon, a tablet was discovered with the equation $x = 2 \cdot \sqrt{3} + 3 \cdot \sqrt{3}$. Simplify the equation.
The Great Pyramid of Giza has a base perimeter of $2 \cdot \sqrt{2} + 2 \cdot \sqrt{3}$ kilometers. Simplify the expression.
The Rosetta Stone has an inscription with the equation $\sqrt{2} \cdot \sqrt{6} = $
A Roman aqueduct has a length of $3 \cdot \sqrt{2}$ meters and a width of $2 \cdot \sqrt{2}$ meters. Find the area.
The ancient Greeks used the equation $\frac{\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{2}} = $
The Hanging Gardens of Babylon had a height of $2 \cdot \sqrt{3}$ meters and a width of $3 \cdot \sqrt{3}$ meters. Find the area.
The ancient Egyptians used the equation $\sqrt{2} \cdot \sqrt{8} = $
The ancient Chinese used the equation $\frac{\sqrt{6}}{\sqrt{2}} = $
The ancient Greeks built a temple with a length of $4 \cdot \sqrt{2}$ meters and a width of $3 \cdot \sqrt{2}$ meters. Find the area and perimeter of the temple.
A Roman engineer designed a bridge with a length of $5 \cdot \sqrt{3}$ meters and a width of $2 \cdot \sqrt{3}$ meters. Find the area and perimeter of the bridge.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Encourage students to use historical contexts to understand the relevance of radicals.
Tip 2: Emphasize the importance of simplifying radical expressions.
Tip 3: Remind students to check their calculations carefully.
